items.artifactContent:
You are a mathematician specialising in equational theories of magmas.
Determine whether Equation 1 implies Equation 2 over all magmas.
TRUE  = every magma satisfying E1 also satisfies E2.
FALSE = some magma satisfies E1 but not E2.
A magma: set M with binary operation *. NO other axioms. Nothing assumed.

Equation 1: {{ equation1 }}
Equation 2: {{ equation2 }}

================================================================
STEP ZERO — MANDATORY FIRST
================================================================
Copy the equations above CHARACTER BY CHARACTER:
  E1: [copy]    vars(E1): [every letter in E1]
  E2: [copy]    vars(E2): [every letter in E2]
  EXTRA: vars in E2 not in E1 = [list or NONE]
  LHS(E1): [the left-hand side of E1, single variable or expression?]
Do NOT paraphrase. Wrong copy = wrong answer.

================================================================
HARD GATES — check in order, STOP at first match
================================================================

GATE 1 — IDENTICAL: E1 and E2 the same equation? → STOP. TRUE.

GATE 2 — FREE VARIABLE (read all conditions before firing):
  Is EXTRA non-empty?
  If NO: skip to GATE 3.
  If YES: check these escape conditions IN ORDER:

  ESCAPE A — RIGHT_ZERO: does E1 have form  x = A * x
    where x is a SINGLE variable AND x is the rightmost factor?
    (Same variable on both sides of the outermost product.)
    If YES: flag RIGHT_ZERO. Extra vars may collapse. Skip to GATE 3.

  ESCAPE B — FREE_RIGHT: does E1 have form  x = A * z
    where x is a SINGLE variable on the LHS (not a compound expression),
    AND z is a DIFFERENT variable appearing ONLY as the rightmost factor,
    AND z does not appear anywhere else in E1?
    If YES: flag FREE_RIGHT. Right argument is absorbed. Skip to GATE 3.

  ESCAPE C — FREE_LEFT_OUTER: does E1 have form  x = z * A
    where x is a SINGLE variable on the LHS,
    AND z is a DIFFERENT variable appearing ONLY as the outermost left factor?
    If YES: flag FREE_LEFT_OUTER. Skip to GATE 3.

  If NONE of A, B, C apply: → STOP. FALSE.
    The extra variable is genuinely unconstrained by E1.
    Left-projection a*b=a is usually the witness — verify it.
    DO NOT enter proof search. This gate is final.

  NOTE: ESCAPE B and C require E1's LHS to be a SINGLE variable x.
  If E1's LHS is a compound expression like x*(x*y), no escape applies.

GATE 3 — TRIVIAL: can you derive x=y from E1 by substitution?
  → STOP. TRUE (any E2 follows from a one-element magma).

GATE 4 — UPC: E1 has form x*y = f(...) with x,y ONLY on left?
  → All products equal. Both sides of E2 are products? STOP. TRUE.

================================================================
MANDATORY WITNESS ELIMINATION — before proof search
================================================================
Attempt all four canonical witnesses. Show work for each.

  W1: a*b = a  (left-projection)
      Does E1 hold for all variable assignments?
      If YES and E2 fails somewhere → STOP. FALSE. Record the case.
      If YES and E2 holds → note and continue.
      If NO → E1 not satisfied, skip.

  W2: a*b = b  (right-projection) — same procedure.
  W3: a*b = 0  (constant-0)       — same procedure.
  W4: a*b = 1  (constant-1)       — same procedure.

After W1–W4, record one of:
  FOUND: "Witness Wn; E2 fails at [values]" → STOP. FALSE.
  NONE_SATISFY_E1: no witness satisfied E1 → E1 is very constrained.
  ALL_HOLD: witnesses that satisfy E1 also satisfy E2 → proceed to proof.

================================================================
PROOF SEARCH (only after witness elimination)
================================================================
A — SPECIALISE: substitute into E1 to obtain E2 directly.
B — REWRITE: apply E1 as rewrite rule on subterms, both directions.
C — SUBSTITUTE: replace a variable with a product, then rewrite.
D — BRIDGE: name the missing sub-identity. Prove E1⟹BRIDGE⟹E2.

REWRITE VALIDITY CHECK — mandatory before each step:
  Quote the current expression.
  Quote the exact subterm that matches LHS(E1) or RHS(E1) verbatim.
  E1 controls ONLY its exact stated pattern — not similar-looking terms,
  not products of different depth, not approximate matches.
  If no subterm matches literally: you CANNOT apply E1 here. Stop.

================================================================
WHEN STUCK: DEFAULT TO FALSE
================================================================
If witness elimination finds no counterexample AND proof search fails:
  Do NOT invent a proof. Do NOT assume TRUE because no witness was found.
  Hard FALSE problems can require counterexamples of size 5 or larger —
  beyond what any manual check can find.
  DEFAULT: output FALSE with reasoning "no proof found; counterexample
  likely exists at larger size."
  Exception: if NONE_SATISFY_E1 (E1 has no models at size 2), then
  E1 is very strongly constraining — lean TRUE and state this explicitly.

================================================================
STRUCTURAL PATTERNS
================================================================
FREE-LEFT: E1 = y*(f(x,...)) with y only as outermost left factor?
  Set y=x: if this forces x*x=x, E2 with only x-products → TRUE.

LEFT-KILL: E1 = A*y = B*y with A free?
  Right-projection a*b=b satisfies E1. E2 needing left→output → FALSE.

ANCHORS (strongest to weakest):
  K:x=y  U:x*y=z*w  L:x=x*y  R:x=y*x  I:x=x*x  C:x*y=y*x
  U = x*x=y*z. L=R in strength. C does NOT imply L.

================================================================
WORKED EXAMPLES
================================================================
FALSE/GATE 2 — free variable, no escapes apply:
  E1: x=x*(y*((z*x)*x))  vars={x,y,z}  LHS=x (single variable)
  E2: x=(y*((z*z)*y))*w  EXTRA={w}
  ESCAPE A: E1 ends in *x? No — ends in *x but check: x=A*x where
    A=x*(y*((z*x)*). Does x appear in A? Yes (inside). Not RIGHT_ZERO.
  ESCAPE B: E1 = x = A*z with z only rightmost? No — rightmost is x, same as LHS.
  ESCAPE C: Not applicable (rightmost, not leftmost escape).
  No escapes. GATE 2 fires. FALSE.

TRUE/FREE_RIGHT — ESCAPE B applies:
  E1: x=(((y*y)*x)*x)*z  vars={x,y,z}  LHS=x (single variable)
  E2: x=y*((x*(y*z))*w)  EXTRA={w}
  ESCAPE B: E1 = x = A*z where A=(((y*y)*x)*x), z appears only as rightmost,
    z does not appear elsewhere in E1, LHS is single variable x. ✓
  Flag FREE_RIGHT. GATE 2 does NOT fire. Continue to witness elimination.
  [Proceed to proof — the free right factor absorbs the extra variable w.]

TRUE/UPC — gate fires immediately:
  E1: x*y=z*(w*(u*u))  x,y only on left → UPC.
  E2: x*(y*y)=(z*w)*z  Both sides products. STOP. TRUE.

TRUE/SPECIALISE — after witnesses:
  E1: x=x*y  E2: x=x*x
  W1: E1→x=x ✓. E2→x=x ✓. W2: E1→x=y, fails. W3: E1→x=0, fails. W4: fails.
  ALL_HOLD (W1 satisfies E1 and E2). PROOF: y→x in E1 gives x=x*x. TRUE.

FALSE/WITNESS — found at W2:
  E1: x*y=((z*w)*u)*y  E2: x=x*((y*x)*(y*z))
  W2 (a*b=b): E1→y=y ✓. E2→x=y*z. Fails at x=0,y=0,z=1. FALSE.

FALSE/STUCK — no witness, no proof:
  W1–W4: none satisfy E1 (or all satisfy both E1 and E2).
  Proof search: no valid rewrite chain found to derive E2 from E1.
  DEFAULT: FALSE. Counterexample likely exists at size > 2.

================================================================
DECISION FLOW
================================================================
  EXTRA vars?  → GATE 2: check escapes A/B/C → FALSE or continue
  Identical?   → TRUE
  Trivial?     → TRUE
  UPC?         → TRUE if E2 sides are products
  W1–W4:       any FALSE witness? → FALSE
               NONE_SATISFY_E1?  → lean TRUE, attempt proof
               ALL_HOLD?         → attempt proof
  Proof found? → TRUE (verify every rewrite step verbatim)
  Stuck?       → DEFAULT FALSE

================================================================
OUTPUT FORMAT — exact headings required for parsing
================================================================
VERDICT: must be exactly TRUE or FALSE (on this line, nothing else)
REASONING: must be non-empty
PROOF: required if VERDICT is TRUE, empty otherwise
COUNTEREXAMPLE: required if VERDICT is FALSE, empty otherwise
items.artifactExcerpt:
You are a mathematician specialising in equational theories of magmas.
Determine whether Equation 1 implies Equation 2 over all magmas.
TRUE  = every magma satisfying E1 also satisfies E2.
FALSE = some magma satisfies E1 but not E2.
A magma: set M with binary operation *. NO other axioms. Nothing…

items.artifactContent:
E1 is {{ equation1 }} and E2 is {{ equation2 }}

TASK: Does E1 imply E2 over all magmas?
DEFAULT: TRUE. Say FALSE only when a test below proves it.

═══ QUICK TRUE ═══
If E1 is literally identical to E2 (ignoring whitespace) → answer TRUE.

═══ STEP 0: SPLIT ON = ═══
Find the = sign. EVERYTHING before it is L, EVERYTHING after it is R.
  E1_L = before = in E1    E1_R = after = in E1
  E2_L = before = in E2    E2_R = after = in E2
Examples:
  "x = (y * x) * z" → L="x", R="(y * x) * z"
  "x * y = (z * x) * x" → L="x * y", R="(z * x) * x"
  "(x * x) * y = (y * x) * z" → L="(x * x) * y", R="(y * x) * z"

═══ STEP 1: STRUCTURAL SEPARATION TESTS ═══
For each test, check E1 and E2 separately.
SEPARATION RULE: E1=HOLD AND E2=FAIL → answer FALSE.
CRITICAL: If E1=FAIL, there is NEVER separation, no matter what E2 is.
  E1=FAIL E2=FAIL → continue.  E1=FAIL E2=HOLD → continue.
  E1=HOLD E2=HOLD → continue.  E1=HOLD E2=FAIL → FALSE.

TEST LP — first letter on L side vs first letter on R side.
  Skip ALL opening ( to find the first letter (a-z).
  Same letter → HOLD. Different letters → FAIL.
  L="(y * x)" → first letter is y. L="((y * x) * z) * w" → y. L="x * y" → x.
  R="(z * (z * x)) * x" → z. R="x * (y * z)" → x.
  Check: E1=HOLD and E2=FAIL? If not → next test.

TEST RP — last letter on L side vs last letter on R side.
  Skip ALL closing ) to find the last letter (a-z).
  Same letter → HOLD. Different letters → FAIL.
  R="(y * x) * z" → last letter is z. R="x * (y * x)" → x. L="x * y" → y.
  Check: E1=HOLD and E2=FAIL? If not → next test.

TEST C0 — does L contain *? Does R contain *?
  Both have * → HOLD. Neither has * and same variable → HOLD. Otherwise → FAIL.
  Check: E1=HOLD and E2=FAIL? If not → next test.

TEST VARS — set of distinct letters on L vs set on R.
  Both have *: same set → HOLD, different set → FAIL.
  One side is bare var v, other has *: HOLD only if * side uses only v.
  Neither has *: same var → HOLD. Otherwise → FAIL.
  Check: E1=HOLD and E2=FAIL? If not → next test.

TEST COUNT2 — for each letter, count occurrences on L and R.
  ALL letters: count_L ≡ count_R mod 2 → HOLD. ANY mismatch → FAIL.
  L="x" {x:1}. R="((y*y)*(y*y))*x" {x:1,y:4}. x:1≡1✓ y:0≡0✓ → HOLD.
  L="x" {x:1}. R="((y*(x*x))*x)*x" {x:4,y:1}. x:1≡0✗ → FAIL.
  Check: E1=HOLD and E2=FAIL? If not → next test.

TEST LDEPTH — left-spine depth: count * nodes going always LEFT from root.
  Bare var→0. "x*y"→1. "(x*y)*z"→2. "x*(y*z)"→1. "((x*y)*z)*w"→3.
  HOLD iff LP=HOLD for this eq AND depth(L) ≡ depth(R) mod 2.
  If LP=FAIL for this eq → LDEPTH=FAIL.
  Check: E1=HOLD and E2=FAIL? If not → STEP 2.

═══ STEP 2: E1 STRENGTH TEST ═══
Skip if E1_L contains * with 2+ variables → go to STEP 2B.
Otherwise (E1_L is a bare variable or has * with 1 variable), continue.

Let SV = the single variable on E1's left side (ignore * if present).
Compute these features of E1_R:

A) NONSV: count ALL occurrences of non-SV variables in E1_R.
   SV=x, R="(y * x) * (z * (w * y))" → y:2+z:1+w:1 = NONSV=4
   SV=x, R="(y * x) * (y * x)" → y:2 = NONSV=2
   SV=x, R="y * (y * (y * x))" → y:3 = NONSV=3

B) SV_STARTS: Is SV the first letter of E1_R (skip parens)?
   SV_ENDS: Is SV the last letter of E1_R (skip parens)?

C) ROOT_CHILD: E1_R has a top-level *. It splits into two parts.
   Is either part a SINGLE non-SV variable?
   "y * (x * x)" → left part is "y" → YES
   "(y * x) * z" → right part is "z" → YES
   "(y * x) * (x * z)" → both compound → NO
   "x * (y * z)" → left part is "x"=SV, not non-SV → NO

D) SV_COUNT: occurrences of SV in E1_R.

E) NVAR: total distinct variables in ALL of E1.

F) MAX_FREQ: highest count of any variable in E1_R.

G) EXTRA_VARS: count of variables in E1_R not appearing in E1_L.

Flowchart — first matching rule wins:
F1: NONSV ≤ 2 AND ROOT_CHILD=NO → FALSE
F2: NONSV ≥ 4 AND SV_COUNT=0 → TRUE
F3: ROOT_CHILD=YES AND NVAR ≥ 3 AND NOT SV_STARTS AND NOT SV_ENDS → TRUE
F4: SV_STARTS or SV_ENDS → FALSE
F5: NONSV ≥ 4 AND EXTRA_VARS ≥ 3 → TRUE
F6: NONSV = 3 AND MAX_FREQ > 2 → FALSE
None match → go to STEP 3.

═══ STEP 2B: COMPOUND LHS ═══
Only when E1_L has * AND 2+ distinct variables.
  If E1_L has exactly 1 * and E1_R has variables not in E1_L → STEP 3.
  Otherwise → FALSE.

═══ STEP 3: T3R RESCUE ═══
Only if RP showed E1=HOLD. If RP showed E1=FAIL → answer TRUE.

T3R magma: a * b = next(b). LEFT input is IGNORED.
  next(0)=1   next(1)=2   next(2)=0

Step A — Assign numbers by first appearance across BOTH equations:
  1st variable = 0, 2nd = 1, 3rd = 2, 4th = 0, 5th = 1.

Step B — Rewrite all variables as numbers.

Step C — Evaluate innermost * first: find (a * b) where both are numbers,
  replace with next(b). Repeat until one number remains per side.

Step D — E1 LHS ≠ E1 RHS → answer TRUE.
  E1 match → evaluate E2.
  E2 match → TRUE. E2 mismatch → FALSE.

═══ OUTPUT ═══
After analysis, write: VERDICT: TRUE or FALSE
IMPORTANT: If you are unsure or cannot complete all tests, answer VERDICT: TRUE.
TRUE is the default — FALSE requires proof.

═══ EXAMPLE A — FALSE via LP ═══
E1: x = x * (y * z)     E2: (x * x) * y = (y * x) * z
LP: E1 first(L)=x first(R)=x → HOLD. E2 first(L)=x first(R)=y → FAIL.
E1=HOLD E2=FAIL → SEPARATION → FALSE.
VERDICT: FALSE

═══ EXAMPLE B — FALSE via C0 ═══
E1: x * y = (y * x) * x     E2: x = (x * y) * z
C0: E1 both sides have * → HOLD. E2 L="x" no *, R has * → FAIL.
E1=HOLD E2=FAIL → SEPARATION → FALSE.
VERDICT: FALSE

═══ EXAMPLE C — FALSE via COUNT2 ═══
E1: x = ((y * y) * (y * y)) * x     E2: x = ((y * (x * x)) * x) * x
LP: E1 x≠y FAIL. E2 x≠y FAIL. Both FAIL → no sep.
RP: E1 x=x HOLD. E2 x=x HOLD. Both HOLD → no sep.
C0: both FAIL. VARS: both FAIL.
COUNT2: E1 L{x:1} R{x:1,y:4}. x:1≡1✓ y:0≡0✓ → HOLD.
  E2 L{x:1} R{x:4,y:1}. x:1≡0✗ → FAIL.
E1=HOLD E2=FAIL → SEPARATION → FALSE.
VERDICT: FALSE

═══ EXAMPLE D — TRUE (no separation found) ═══
E1: x = (y * x) * (z * (w * y))     E2: x = ((y * x) * x) * x
LP: E1 x≠y FAIL. E2 x≠y FAIL. Both FAIL → no sep (E1=FAIL means no sep).
RP: E1 x≠y FAIL. E2 x=x HOLD. E1=FAIL → no sep.
C0: both FAIL. VARS: both FAIL. COUNT2: both FAIL. LDEPTH: both FAIL.
STRENGTH: SV=x. NONSV=4 (y:2+z:1+w:1). SV_COUNT=1. SV_STARTS=no. SV_ENDS=no.
EXTRA_VARS={y,z,w}=3. F5: NONSV≥4 AND EXTRA≥3 → TRUE.
VERDICT: TRUE

═══ EXAMPLE E — FALSE via STRENGTH F4 ═══
E1: x = x * (y * (x * y))     E2: x = (y * x) * (y * (y * x))
LP: E1 x=x HOLD. E2 x≠y FAIL. E1=HOLD E2=FAIL? No — E2=FAIL but E1=HOLD...
Wait: LP E1=HOLD, E2=FAIL → SEPARATION → FALSE already via LP!
But ALSO strength: SV=x, SV_STARTS=yes → F4 → FALSE.
VERDICT: FALSE

═══ EXAMPLE F — FALSE via T3R ═══
E1: x = y * (y * (x * x))     E2: x = (x * (y * z)) * x
Structural tests: no separation (LP both FAIL, RP both HOLD, etc.).
STRENGTH: SV=x, NONSV=2, ROOT_CHILD: left="y"→YES. F1 skipped (ROOT_CHILD=YES).
  SV_ENDS=yes (last letter of R is x) → F4 → FALSE.
Also T3R confirms: vars x=0,y=1,z=2.
  E1 RHS: 1*(1*(0*0)): 0*0→1, 1*1→2, 1*2→0. E1: 0=0 ✓
  E2 RHS: (0*(1*2))*0: 1*2→0, 0*0→1, 1*0→1. E2: 0≠1 → FALSE.
VERDICT: FALSE

TASK:
Determine whether  {{ equation1 }} implies {{ equation2 }} using ONLY strict ZPYPIPE THEN follow [#Final_Step] For Final Verdict…

items.artifactContent:
Decide whether source law A ({{ equation1 }}) implies target law B ({{ equation2 }}) over all magmas (binary op *). Follow this protocol exactly.

IMPORTANT

- Never override a rule with free-form algebra.
- If a source-collapse lemma, contradiction motif, separator, or FALSE rule fires, stop immediately.
- Only use extra algebra for the source-collapse lemmas in STEP 1.
- If no listed rule proves TRUE, answer FALSE.

Rule: a single valid counterexample is decisive; no probability adjustment can override it.

STEP 1: SOURCE-COLLAPSE LEMMAS
First check whether A is a bare law: one side is a single variable and the other side is a product term.

Only for bare laws, normalize A as follows:
- swap the sides if needed so the single variable is on the LEFT
- rename that bare variable to x
- on the product side, keep full bracketing
- every occurrence of the bare variable stays x
- rename OTHER variables by first appearance on the product side to y, then z, then w, ...

After this normalization, compare A to exactly one of these canonical source laws:
- x=x*(y*(z*(x*y))) -> LEFT PROJECTION: p*q=p
- x=x*((y*z)*(z*z)) -> LEFT PROJECTION: p*q=p
- x=(((y*z)*x)*z)*x -> RIGHT PROJECTION: p*q=q

Examples:
- a=a*((b*c)*(c*c)) normalizes to x=x*((y*z)*(z*z))
- m=(((n*p)*m)*p)*m normalizes to x=(((y*z)*x)*z)*x

If a collapse lemma fires, evaluate B under that projection and stop:
- under LEFT PROJECTION, every product collapses to its leftmost variable
- under RIGHT PROJECTION, every product collapses to its rightmost variable
- if both sides of B collapse to the same variable, answer TRUE; otherwise FALSE

NON-BARE CONSTANT-PRODUCT LEMMA

Also check this non-bare source family:
- normalize A by renaming variables by first appearance, but keep full bracketing
- if normalized A has LEFT side exactly x*y
- and the RIGHT side is a product term U * V
- and every occurrence of x on the RIGHT side lies in V and none lies in U

then use CONSTANT-PRODUCT collapse:
- every non-variable product term collapses to the same symbol c
- a single variable stays itself
- if both sides of B collapse to the same symbol, answer TRUE; otherwise FALSE

Example:
- x*y=y*((z*w)*x) fits this family
- under CONSTANT-PRODUCT collapse, both ((z*x)*y)*x and x*x collapse to c

STEP 1B: BARE-SOURCE CONTRADICTION MOTIFS

Apply this step only if A is bare and STEP 1 did not fire.

For STEP 1B, do not do a full rename.
- only swap sides if needed so the single variable side is on the LEFT
- then work on the PRODUCT side exactly as written
- treat the lone variable on the LEFT as the distinguished variable for this step

Compute these source-only features of the product side:
- rhsVars(A) = number of distinct variables on the product side
- rhsTotals(A) = sorted occurrence counts on the product side only, written without separators
- reminders for rhsTotals(A):
  - if the counts are 2,2,1, then rhsTotals=122, never 221
  - if the counts are 3,1,1, then rhsTotals=113
  - if the counts are 2,1,1,1, then rhsTotals=1112
- Lx(A) = whether the leftmost variable on the product side is x
- Rx(A) = whether the rightmost variable on the product side is x
- xTop(A):
  - left if, at the top product split U*V, the variable x occurs only in U
  - right if x occurs only in V
  - both if x occurs in both U and V
- square(A) = whether the product side contains a repeated square subterm like u*u
- topShape(A):
  - v-m if the product side is (variable * product)
  - m-v if it is (product * variable)
  - m-m if both top children are products

Worked examples:
- x=y*((z*x)*z)
  product side uses variables {x,y,z}, so rhsVars=3
  product-side counts are x:1, y:1, z:2, so rhsTotals=112
  leftmost is y, rightmost is z, so Lx=FALSE, Rx=FALSE
  at the top split y * ((z*x)*z), the variable x occurs only on the RIGHT, so xTop=right
  topShape=v-m
- x = ((y*(x*x))*z)*w
  product side has rhsVars=4, and neither leftmost nor rightmost variable is x
- x = x*(y*((x*z)*y))
  product-side counts are x:2, y:2, z:1, so rhsTotals=122
  at the top split x * (y*((x*z)*y)), the variable x occurs in BOTH top children, so xTop=both

If any contradiction motif matches, answer TRUE immediately:
- C1: if rhsVars(A)=4 and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=FALSE, answer TRUE
- C2: if the product side has exactly three variables with counts 3,1,1, and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=FALSE, answer TRUE
- C3: if Lx(A)=FALSE and xTop(A)=left and square(A)=TRUE and topShape(A)=m-v, answer TRUE
- C4: if the product side has exactly three variables with counts 2,1,1, and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=FALSE and xTop(A)=right and topShape(A)=v-m, answer TRUE
- C5: if the product side has exactly four variables with counts 2,1,1,1, and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=FALSE and xTop(A)=right and topShape(A)=v-m, answer TRUE
- C6: if rhsVars(A)=3 and xTop(A)=right and topShape(A)=v-m and the product side contains exactly two occurrences of x, answer TRUE
- C7: if rhsVars(A)=3 and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=FALSE and xTop(A)=left and topShape(A)=m-v and the product side contains exactly two occurrences of x, answer TRUE
- C8: if rhsVars(A)=3 and Lx(A)=TRUE and xTop(A)=left and topShape(A)=m-v, answer TRUE
- C9: if the product side has exactly three variables with counts 2,2,1, and Lx(A)=TRUE and Rx(A)=FALSE and xTop(A)=both and topShape(A)=v-m, answer TRUE
- C10: if the product side has exactly three variables with counts 2,2,1, and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=TRUE and the product side contains exactly two occurrences of x, answer TRUE
- C11: if the product side has exactly three variables with counts 3,1,1, and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=FALSE and xTop(A)=right and topShape(A)=v-m, answer TRUE
- C12: if the product side has exactly three variables with counts 3,1,1, and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=FALSE and xTop(A)=left and topShape(A)=m-v, answer TRUE
- C13: if the product side has exactly four variables with counts 2,1,1,1, and Lx(A)=FALSE and Rx(A)=TRUE, answer TRUE
- C14: if B is bare, and the product side has exactly three variables with counts 3,1,1, and Rx(A)=TRUE and square(A)=FALSE, answer TRUE

STEP 2: COMPUTE FEATURES EXACTLY

For each equation E: L = R, count variable letters on each side separately.

WORKED EXAMPLE: x=y*(y*((x*y)*x))
- left side variables: [x] so left count = 1
- right side variables: [y, y, x, y, x] so right count = 5
- size(E) = 1 + 5 = 6
- vars(E) = 2 because only {x, y}
- imb(E) = |1-2| + |0-3| = 4
- bare(E) = TRUE because one side is a single variable
- LP(E) = FALSE because leftmost vars are x and y
- RP(E) = TRUE because rightmost vars are both x
- SET(E) = FALSE because left uses {x} and right uses {x,y}
- XOR(E) = FALSE because parity does not match
- AB(E) = FALSE because exact counts do not match

Compute for both A and B:
- size(E) = total variable occurrences on both sides
- vars(E) = number of distinct variables
- imb(E) = sum of absolute left/right count differences
- bare(E) = one side is a single variable
- LP(E) = leftmost variable matches across the two sides
- RP(E) = rightmost variable matches across the two sides
- SET(E) = both sides use the same variable set
- XOR(E) = every variable has the same parity on both sides
- AB(E) = every variable has exactly the same count on both sides

Reminder for vars(E):
- count distinct variable LETTERS only
- multiplicity does not matter
- example: x*y=(x*(x*z))*x has vars=3 because the set is {x,y,z}

STEP 3: SEPARATOR TESTS

If LP(A) and not LP(B): FALSE.
If RP(A) and not RP(B): FALSE.
If SET(A) and not SET(B): FALSE.
If XOR(A) and not XOR(B): FALSE.
If AB(A) and not AB(B): FALSE.

One separator means FALSE immediately.

STEP 4: FALSE GUARD

G1: If all of these hold, answer FALSE:
- bare(A) is true
- bare(B) is false
- size(A) = size(B)
- vars(B) < vars(A)
- imb(B) > imb(A)
- XOR(A) is true

G2: If all of these hold, answer FALSE:
- bare(A) is true
- vars(A) = 2
- bare(B) is false
- vars(B) > vars(A)
- size(B) >= size(A)

G3: If all of these hold, answer FALSE:
- bare(A) is false
- vars(A) >= 5
- bare(B) is false
- vars(B) < vars(A)
- size(B) = size(A)

G4: If all of these hold, answer FALSE:
- bare(A) is false
- bare(B) is false
- size(B) = size(A)
- vars(B) <= vars(A) - 2

STEP 5: STRUCTURAL RULES

Let sA=size(A), sB=size(B), vA=vars(A), vB=vars(B), iA=imb(A), iB=imb(B).

Apply in order, stopping at the first match:
- T4: if bare(A) is true and vA >= 4 and vB = 2, answer TRUE.
- T6: if bare(A) is true and RP(A) is false and XOR(A) is true and vB = 2 and RP(B) is true, answer TRUE.
- T7: if bare(A) is false and vA = 3 and iA = 2 and vB = 3 and XOR(B) is false, answer TRUE.
- T8: if vA = 3 and XOR(A) is false and bare(B) is true and RP(B) is false, answer TRUE.
- F1: if vB = vA, answer FALSE.
- F2: if iB < iA, answer FALSE.
- F3: if sB < sA, answer FALSE.
- T1: if bare(A) is true and vA >= 3 and sB > sA, answer TRUE.
- T2: if bare(A) is true and bare(B) is true and vA >= 3 and vB > vA and iB > iA, answer TRUE.
- T5: if vB < vA and iB > iA, answer TRUE.

STEP 6: EXTENDED RULES

Apply only if nothing above fired.

- D3: if every variable in A appears the same TOTAL number of times across both sides, and vB < vA, and B is a bare law of the form x = x * (...), answer TRUE.
- D4: otherwise FALSE.

Example for D3:
- x=((y*x)*y)*(z*z) has total counts x:2, y:2, z:2, so the totals are uniform

REASONING STYLE

State:
- the computed features,
- whether a source-collapse lemma or contradiction motif fired,
- whether any separator or guard fired,
- and which final rule decided the verdict.

Be concise. If no listed rule proves TRUE, answer FALSE.

OUTPUT FORMAT (use exact headers, no extra formatting):
VERDICT: TRUE or FALSE
REASONING: non-empty explanation
PROOF: required if VERDICT is TRUE, empty otherwise
COUNTEREXAMPLE: required if VERDICT is FALSE, empty otherwise

Decide whether source law A ({{ equation1 }}) implies target law B ({{ equation2 }}) over all magmas (binary op *). Follow this protocol exactly.

IMPORTANT

- Never override a rule with free-form algebra.
- If a source-collapse lemma, contradiction motif, separator, or FALSE rule fires, stop…

items.artifactContent:
<task>
Decide whether Eq1 implies Eq2 over all magmas.
A magma is a set with one binary operation `*`.
Do NOT assume associativity, commutativity, identity, cancellation, inverses, order, constants, or any law not forced by Eq1.
Variables are single lowercase letters. Constants do not occur in the input.
Work offline from this text only. Do not use tools.
</task>

<input>
Eq1: {{ equation1 }}
Eq2: {{ equation2 }}
</input>

<syntax>
A term is a binary tree: term := variable OR (term * term).
Outer parentheses may be omitted. When a chain has no parentheses, parse `*` left-associatively: x*y*z means ((x*y)*z). Parentheses override this, so x*(y*z) means (x*(y*z)).
Never reassociate: (x*y)*z and x*(y*z) are different terms.
An equation has left side Eq.L and right side Eq.R.
</syntax>

<output>
Output exactly two lines and nothing else:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: <rule_id>
or
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: <rule_id>

Only TRUE or FALSE are allowed as verdicts. Never output explanations, headers, examples, or a third line.
</output>

<procedure>
Apply rules in order. A named TRUE rule is allowed only when its precondition is exactly verified. A named FALSE rule is allowed only when its witness magma makes Eq1 true for all assignments and Eq2 false for some assignment.

## STEP 1: Extract Features
For each side s:
- leftmost(s): first variable letter read left-to-right.
- rightmost(s): last variable letter read left-to-right.
- bare(s): true iff s is one variable and contains no `*`.
- vars(s): set of variables in s. vars(eq) = vars(eq.L) union vars(eq.R).
- parity(s,v): number of occurrences of v in s, mod 2.
- occ(s): total number of variable occurrences in s.
- left_depth(s): number of `*` nodes on the path root -> left child -> left child ... until a variable.
  Examples: left_depth(x)=0, left_depth(x*y)=1, left_depth((x*y)*z)=2, left_depth(x*(y*z))=1.
- right_depth(s): same on the repeated right-child path.
  Examples: right_depth(x)=0, right_depth(x*y)=1, right_depth(x*(y*z))=2, right_depth((x*y)*z)=1.

## STEP 2: Immediate TRUE Rules

T1. Eq2 is a tautology: Eq2.L and Eq2.R are the same parsed term. Ignore spaces and redundant outer parentheses only.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T1

T2. Eq2 is exactly Eq1 with the two sides swapped and no other change.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T2

T3. Eq2 is Eq1 after a bijective variable renaming, possibly with Eq2 sides swapped.
Normalization test for an oriented equation A=B:
- Scan A left-to-right, then B left-to-right.
- For variable order, ignore spaces, parentheses, `*`, and `=`.
- Replace the first new variable by v1, the next by v2, then v3, v4, ...
- Keep the full tree structure: parentheses and every `*` position must still match. Same variable sequence is not enough.
Normalize Eq1.L=Eq1.R. Compare it with normalized Eq2.L=Eq2.R and also normalized Eq2.R=Eq2.L. If either is identical, T3 fires.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T3

T4. Eq2 is an instance of Eq1 by substituting one fixed term for each variable of Eq1, possibly with Eq2 sides swapped.
Different variables of Eq1 may map to the same target term. Example: x*y=x matches z*z=z with x->z and y->z.
Use this mechanical matcher on Eq1 against a target equation A=B.
- Match Eq1.L with A and Eq1.R with B using one shared assignment map.
- If an Eq1 node is a variable v, record v -> the whole target subterm at that position. If v was already recorded differently, the match fails.
- If an Eq1 node is P*Q, the target node must also be X*Y. Then match P with X and Q with Y.
- The match succeeds only if all repeated variables of Eq1 use the same target term everywhere.
First try target Eq2.L=Eq2.R. If it fails, try target Eq2.R=Eq2.L. If either succeeds, T4 fires.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T4

T5. Eq1 has the form u=v where u and v are any two distinct single variables, such as x=y or a=b. Then all elements are equal, so every Eq2 follows.
Do not apply T5 to product equations such as x*x=y.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T5

T6. Context replacement: Eq1 applied once inside a surrounding context of Eq2.
- Compare Eq2.L and Eq2.R as parsed trees, position by position from the root.
- If all corresponding positions are identical except exactly ONE subterm position where Eq2.L has subterm U and Eq2.R has subterm W:
  - Apply T4's mechanical matcher to check if (U, W) matches target (Eq1.L, Eq1.R).
  - Also try the reversed direction: check if (U, W) matches target (Eq1.R, Eq1.L).
  - If either direction matches with a consistent assignment, T6 fires.
- If Eq2.L and Eq2.R are identical (T1 should have fired) or differ at more than one subterm position, T6 does NOT fire.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T6

## STEP 3: FALSE Rules by Witness Magmas

For every FALSE rule below, both conditions are required: Witness for Eq1 must be true AND Failure for Eq2 must be true. If either condition fails, skip that rule. Never output FALSE just because Eq2 looks different from Eq1.
Variables appearing only in Eq2 are allowed. In a counterexample, any Eq2 variable can be assigned freely in the witness set; Eq1 still only needs to hold for all assignments to its variables.

L1. LEFT PROJECTION on {0,1}: a*b = a. Every product term evaluates to its leftmost variable.
Witness: leftmost(Eq1.L) == leftmost(Eq1.R).
Failure: leftmost(Eq2.L) != leftmost(Eq2.R).
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: L1

L2. RIGHT PROJECTION on {0,1}: a*b = b. Every product term evaluates to its rightmost variable.
Witness: rightmost(Eq1.L) == rightmost(Eq1.R).
Failure: rightmost(Eq2.L) != rightmost(Eq2.R).
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: L2

L3. CONSTANT ZERO on {0,1}: a*b = 0. Every product evaluates to 0; a bare variable evaluates to itself. Here 0 is an element of the witness set {0,1}, not a constant symbol in Eq1 or Eq2.
Define cf(eq): true iff (eq.L is not bare AND eq.R is not bare) OR (eq.L and eq.R are the same bare variable).
Witness: cf(Eq1) is true.
Failure: cf(Eq2) is false.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: L3

M1. XOR on {0,1}: a*b = (a+b) mod 2. A term evaluates to the XOR of all variable occurrences.
Use M1 only if each of Eq1.L, Eq1.R, Eq2.L, Eq2.R has occ <= 8. If any side has occ > 8, M1 is forbidden: skip M1 entirely; do not estimate parity.
Witness: for every v in vars(Eq1), parity(Eq1.L,v) == parity(Eq1.R,v).
Failure: for some v in vars(Eq2), parity(Eq2.L,v) != parity(Eq2.R,v).
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: M1

M6. LEFT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1-a. A term evaluates to leftmost variable XOR (left_depth mod 2).
Witness: leftmost(Eq1.L) == leftmost(Eq1.R) AND left_depth(Eq1.L) mod 2 == left_depth(Eq1.R) mod 2.
Failure: leftmost(Eq2.L) != leftmost(Eq2.R) OR left_depth(Eq2.L) mod 2 != left_depth(Eq2.R) mod 2.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: M6

M7. RIGHT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1-b. A term evaluates to rightmost variable XOR (right_depth mod 2).
Witness: rightmost(Eq1.L) == rightmost(Eq1.R) AND right_depth(Eq1.L) mod 2 == right_depth(Eq1.R) mod 2.
Failure: rightmost(Eq2.L) != rightmost(Eq2.R) OR right_depth(Eq2.L) mod 2 != right_depth(Eq2.R) mod 2.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: M7

## STEP 4: Final Safety Scan and Fallback
Before DEFAULT, re-run exactly T1, T2, T3, T4, T5, and T6 with the swapped Eq2 orientation where the rule allows it. Do not use any other TRUE intuition such as associativity, commutativity, or cancellation.
If no rule fired, the case is unresolved by this procedure. DEFAULT is a forced fallback only, not evidence for TRUE or FALSE.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: DEFAULT

## Guards
- Parentheses and tree shape matter. Never commute, reassociate, cancel, or simplify.
- For FALSE rules L1,L2,L3,M1,M6,M7, Eq1 Witness and Eq2 Failure must both pass exactly.
- For TRUE rules T1-T6, similarity is not enough; the exact test must pass.
- Do not invent rules. Do not guess from similarity or from Eq2 looking simpler, longer, or different.
- DEFAULT is only a forced unresolved fallback.
</procedure>

<examples>
Example TRUE by T4 (repeated target term):
Eq1: x*y = x
Eq2: z*z = z
Match Eq1.L=x*y against Eq2.L=z*z: x -> z, y -> z. Match Eq1.R=x against Eq2.R=z: x -> z (consistent). T4 fires.
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T4

Non-example for T4 (repeated variable inconsistency):
Eq1: x*x = x
Eq2: a*b = a
Match Eq1.L=x*x against Eq2.L=a*b: first x -> a, second x -> b. Inconsistent. T4 fails for the direct direction; the swapped direction also fails. Continue to T6, then to L1 etc.

Example TRUE by T6 (one-context replacement):
Eq1: x*x = x
Eq2: (a*a)*b = a*b
Compare Eq2.L=(a*a)*b and Eq2.R=a*b as trees. Both are of the form X*b; they differ only at the left child: Eq2.L has (a*a), Eq2.R has a. Match (a*a, a) against target (Eq1.L, Eq1.R) = (x*x, x) with x -> a: consistent. T6 fires.
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T6

Example FALSE by L1 (left projection witness):
Eq1: x*y = x
Eq2: x*y = y
L1 Witness: leftmost(Eq1.L)=x, leftmost(Eq1.R)=x. Equal.
L1 Failure: leftmost(Eq2.L)=x, leftmost(Eq2.R)=y. Different.
L1 fires.
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: L1
</examples>

- rightmost(s): last variable letter read left-to-right.
- bare(s): true iff s is one variable and contains no `*`.
- vars(s): set of variables in s. vars(eq) = vars(eq.L) union vars(eq.R).
- parity(s,v): number of occurrences of v in s, mod 2.
- occ(s): total number of variable occurrences in s.
- left_depth(s): number of `*` nodes on the path root -> left child -> left child ... until a variable.
  Examples: left_depth(x)=0, left_depth(x*y)=1, left_depth((x*y)*z)=2, left_depth(x*(y*z))=1.
- right_depth(s): same on the repeated right-child path.
  Examples: right_depth(x)=0, right_depth(x*y)=1, right_depth(x*(y*z))=2, right_depth((x*y)*z)=1.

## STEP 2: Immediate TRUE Rules

T1. Eq2 is a tautology: Eq2.L and Eq2.R are the same parsed term. Ignore spaces and redundant outer parentheses only.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T1

T2. Eq2 is exactly Eq1 with the two sides swapped and no other change.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T2

T3. Eq2 is Eq1 after a bijective variable renaming, possibly with Eq2 sides swapped.
Normalization test for an oriented equation A=B:
- Scan A left-to-right, then B left-to-right.
- For variable order, ignore spaces, parentheses, `*`, and `=`.
- Replace the first new variable by a, the next by b, then c, d, e.
- Keep the full tree structure: parentheses and every `*` position must still match. Same variable sequence is not enough.
Normalize Eq1.L=Eq1.R. Compare it with normalized Eq2.L=Eq2.R and also normalized Eq2.R=Eq2.L. If either is identical, T3 fires.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T3

T4. Eq2 is an instance of Eq1 by substituting one fixed term for each variable of Eq1, possibly with Eq2 sides swapped.
Different variables of Eq1 may map to the same target term. Example: x*y=x matches z*z=z with x->z and y->z.
Use this mechanical matcher on Eq1 against a target equation A=B.
- Match Eq1.L with A and Eq1.R with B using one shared assignment map.
- If an Eq1 node is a variable v, record v -> the whole target subterm at that position. If v was already recorded differently, the match fails.
- If an Eq1 node is P*Q, the target node must also be X*Y. Then match P with X and Q with Y.
- The match succeeds only if all repeated variables of Eq1 use the same target term everywhere.
First try target Eq2.L=Eq2.R. If it fails, try target Eq2.R=Eq2.L. If either succeeds, T4 fires.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T4

T5. Eq1 has the form u=v where u and v are any two distinct single variables, such as x=y or a=b. Then all elements are equal, so every Eq2 follows.
Do not apply T5 to product equations such as x*x=y.
Output:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: T5

## STEP 3: FALSE Rules by Witness Magmas

For every FALSE rule below, both conditions are required: Witness for Eq1 must be true AND Failure for Eq2 must be true. If either condition fails, skip that rule. Never output FALSE just because Eq2 looks different from Eq1.

L1. LEFT PROJECTION on {0,1}: a*b = a. Every product term evaluates to its leftmost variable.
Witness: leftmost(Eq1.L) == leftmost(Eq1.R).
Failure: leftmost(Eq2.L) != leftmost(Eq2.R).
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: L1

L2. RIGHT PROJECTION on {0,1}: a*b = b. Every product term evaluates to its rightmost variable.
Witness: rightmost(Eq1.L) == rightmost(Eq1.R).
Failure: rightmost(Eq2.L) != rightmost(Eq2.R).
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: L2

L3. CONSTANT ZERO on {0,1}: a*b = 0. Every product evaluates to 0; a bare variable evaluates to itself.
Define cf(eq): true iff (eq.L is not bare AND eq.R is not bare) OR (eq.L and eq.R are the same bare variable).
Witness: cf(Eq1) is true.
Failure: cf(Eq2) is false.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: L3

M1. XOR on {0,1}: a*b = (a+b) mod 2. A term evaluates to the XOR of all variable occurrences.
Use M1 only if each of Eq1.L, Eq1.R, Eq2.L, Eq2.R has occ <= 8. If any side has occ > 8, M1 is forbidden: skip M1 entirely; do not estimate parity.
Witness: for every v in vars(Eq1), parity(Eq1.L,v) == parity(Eq1.R,v).
Failure: for some v in vars(Eq2), parity(Eq2.L,v) != parity(Eq2.R,v).
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: M1

M6. LEFT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1-a. A term evaluates to leftmost variable XOR (left_depth mod 2).
Witness: leftmost(Eq1.L) == leftmost(Eq1.R) AND left_depth(Eq1.L) mod 2 == left_depth(Eq1.R) mod 2.
Failure: leftmost(Eq2.L) != leftmost(Eq2.R) OR left_depth(Eq2.L) mod 2 != left_depth(Eq2.R) mod 2.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: M6

M7. RIGHT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1-b. A term evaluates to rightmost variable XOR (right_depth mod 2).
Witness: rightmost(Eq1.L) == rightmost(Eq1.R) AND right_depth(Eq1.L) mod 2 == right_depth(Eq1.R) mod 2.
Failure: rightmost(Eq2.L) != rightmost(Eq2.R) OR right_depth(Eq2.L) mod 2 != right_depth(Eq2.R) mod 2.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: M7

## STEP 4: Final Safety Scan and Fallback
Before DEFAULT, re-check all obvious TRUE rules: T1, T2, T3, T4, and T5, including swapped Eq2 orientation where the rule allows it.
If no rule fired, the case is unresolved by this procedure. DEFAULT is a forced fallback only, not evidence for TRUE or FALSE. Do not say failure to find a proof or counterexample proves anything.
Output:
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: DEFAULT

## Guards
- Parentheses and tree shape matter. Never commute, reassociate, cancel, or simplify.
- For FALSE rules L1,L2,L3,M1,M6,M7, Eq1 Witness and Eq2 Failure must both pass exactly.
- For TRUE rules T1-T5, similarity is not enough; the exact test must pass.
- Do not invent rules. Do not guess from similarity or from Eq2 looking simpler, longer, or different.
- DEFAULT is only a forced unresolved fallback.
</procedure>

Variables are single lowercase letters. Constants do not occur in the input.
Work offline from this text only. Do not use tools.
</task>

<input>
Eq1: {{ equation1 }}
Eq2: {{ equation2 }}
</input>

<syntax>
A term is a binary tree: term := variable OR (term * term).
Outer parentheses may be omitted. When a chain has no parentheses, parse `*` left-associatively: x*y*z means ((x*y)*z). Parentheses override this, so x*(y*z) means (x*(y*z)).
Never reassociate: (x*y)*z and x*(y*z) are different terms.
An equation has left side Eq.L and right side Eq.R.
</syntax>

<output>
Output exactly two lines and nothing else:
VERDICT: TRUE
RULE: <rule_id>
or
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: <rule_id>

Only TRUE or FALSE are allowed as verdicts. Never output explanations, headers, examples, or a third line.
</output>

<procedure>

items.artifactContent:
<task>
You are a strict symbolic procedure runner.

You must execute the procedure exactly in order.
</task>

<input>
EQ1 = {{ equation1 }}
EQ2 = {{ equation2 }}
</input>

<procedure>
STEP 1.
Parse EQ1 and EQ2 as binary trees.
- Every variable symbol is an atom.
- Every binary operator symbol is the same generic binary operation.
- Parentheses override grouping.
- Otherwise read binary expressions left-associatively.
- Treat any non-variable infix symbol such as *, ◇, ·, ○, or ∘ as that same generic binary operation.

STEP 2.
Compute exactly these quantities and no others.

1. eq1_lhs_is_var
   1 iff LHS(EQ1) is a single variable, else 0.
2. eq1_var_count
   Number of distinct variables appearing anywhere in EQ1.
3. delta_rhs_distinct
   Distinct RHS variable count in EQ2 minus distinct RHS variable count in EQ1.
4. side_coupling_delta
   Cross-side variable coupling score of EQ2 minus that of EQ1, where one equation's coupling score is (# variables appearing on both sides) / (# variables appearing anywhere in that equation).
5. lhs_skeleton_eq
   1 iff LHS(EQ1) and LHS(EQ2) have the same tree skeleton, else 0.
6. delta_rhs_nodes
   RHS node count in EQ2 minus RHS node count in EQ1.
7. delta_lhs_nodes
   LHS node count in EQ2 minus LHS node count in EQ1.
8. new_total_vars_count
   Number of distinct variables appearing in EQ2 but not in EQ1.
9. eq1_rhs_distinct
   Number of distinct variables appearing in RHS(EQ1).

STEP 3.
Compute the regime_code using ONLY these exact rules.
- lhs_flag = "L1" if eq1_lhs_is_var >= 1, else "L0"
- var_bucket = "vle2" if eq1_var_count <= 2, else "v3" if eq1_var_count == 3, else "vge4"
- lhs_node_bucket = "dneg2" if delta_lhs_nodes <= -2, else "dmid" if delta_lhs_nodes <= 0, else "dpos"
- regime_code = lhs_flag + "_" + var_bucket + "_" + lhs_node_bucket

STEP 4.
Apply regime-specific blocks first.
If a listed regime_code matches, use ONLY that block.
Otherwise use the GLOBAL block.
Inside any active block, use the first matching rule.

BLOCK regime_code == "L1_vge4_dmid"
R1. if delta_lhs_nodes >= 0 and side_coupling_delta >= -0.083333 then answer = "1"
R2. if delta_lhs_nodes >= 0 and new_total_vars_count <= 0 then answer = "1"
If no rule matched in this block, answer = "0"

BLOCK regime_code == "L1_v3_dmid"
R1. if delta_rhs_distinct >= 1 then answer = "1"
R2. if eq1_rhs_distinct <= 3 and side_coupling_delta <= 0.083333 then answer = "0"
R3. if delta_lhs_nodes >= 0 and side_coupling_delta >= 0 then answer = "1"
If no rule matched in this block, answer = "0"

BLOCK regime_code == "L1_v3_dpos"

items.artifactContent:
Decide whether Eq1 implies Eq2 over all magmas (set + binary op *).
Do NOT assume associativity, commutativity, or any algebraic law.

Eq1: {{ equation1 }}
Eq2: {{ equation2 }}

Apply rules in order. First match wins. If no rule fires → TRUE.

Definitions:
  leftmost(s)    = first variable letter in s, L→R
  rightmost(s)   = last variable letter in s
  bare(s)        = TRUE iff s is a single variable letter
  parity(s,v)    = # times v appears in s, mod 2
  left-depth(s)  = # * nodes on path root → left-child → left-child → …
  right-depth(s) = same on the right

═══ FALSE via 2-element witness magmas ═══

L1. LEFT PROJECTION (a*b = a). Every term evaluates to its leftmost
    variable.
    leftmost(Eq1.L) = leftmost(Eq1.R)
    AND leftmost(Eq2.L) ≠ leftmost(Eq2.R) → FALSE.

L2. RIGHT PROJECTION (a*b = b). Mirror of L1 using rightmost.
    rightmost(Eq1.L) = rightmost(Eq1.R)
    AND rightmost(Eq2.L) ≠ rightmost(Eq2.R) → FALSE.

L3. CONSTANT ZERO (a*b = 0). Products evaluate to 0; bare variables
    evaluate to themselves. Let cf(eq) = "both sides are non-bare
    products, OR both sides are the SAME single variable letter."
    cf(Eq1) AND NOT cf(Eq2) → FALSE.

═══ FALSE via semantic evaluation in small concrete magmas ═══

For each magma below: first check that Eq1 HOLDS in it under every
variable assignment. Then check whether Eq2 FAILS under some
assignment. If both, answer FALSE. Do NOT force FALSE without a
concrete counterexample.

M1. XOR on {0,1}: a*b = (a+b) mod 2.
    Closed form: a term's value = parity-sum (mod 2) of its variables.
    Equation holds iff, for EVERY variable v in the equation,
      parity(L, v) = parity(R, v).

    Ex: Eq1 = (x*y)*(x*y) = z*z.
        x: L=2→0, R=0. y: same. z: L=0, R=2→0. All match → Eq1 holds.
        Now check Eq2 under the same parity rule.

M2. LEFT-CYCLE on {0,1,2}: a*b = (a+1) mod 3.
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides share
      (i) the same leftmost variable, AND
      (ii) the same left-depth mod 3.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = ((x*y)*z)*w.
        leftmost: x vs x ✓. left-depth: 0 vs 3; 0 mod 3 = 0, 3 mod 3 = 0 ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M3. RIGHT-CYCLE on {0,1,2}: a*b = (b+1) mod 3.
    Mirror of M2 using rightmost + right-depth.
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides share rightmost variable AND
    right-depth mod 3.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = y * (z * (w * x)).
        rightmost: x vs x ✓. right-depth: 0 vs 3; 0 mod 3 = 0, 3 mod 3 = 0 ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M4. SPIKE-ZERO on {0,1,2}: 0*1 = 2, every other product = 0.
    A term is nonzero only if it contains a subterm of shape (x*y)
    where x, y are bare variables and, under assignment, x=0, y=1.
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides reduce to 0 under every assignment
    (e.g. both sides are non-bare products whose outer-left operand is
    NOT a bare-variable product). It FAILS if one side is a bare
    variable and the other is a product.

    Ex: Eq1 = (x*y)*z = (a*b)*c. Both outer products have non-bare
    left operand, so both sides = 0 universally → Eq1 holds. Now
    look for a spike-trigger on one side of Eq2.

M6. LEFT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1 − a.
    Each * flips the left child's value. Term value = leftmost variable
    XOR (left-depth mod 2).
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides share leftmost variable AND
    left-depth(L) ≡ left-depth(R) (mod 2).

    Ex: Eq1 = x = (x * y) * z.
        leftmost: x vs x ✓. left-depth: 0 (even) vs 2 (even) ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M7. RIGHT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1 − b.
    Mirror of M6 using rightmost + right-depth parity.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = y * (z * x).
        rightmost: x vs x ✓. right-depth: 0 (even) vs 2 (even) ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M8. CUSTOM ABSORPTION MAGMA. Use ONLY when BOTH:
    (a) both sides of Eq1 contain ≥1 *;
    (b) at least one side's outer * has a bare variable on one side and
        a composite on the other, where that bare variable appears
        NOWHERE else in Eq1.
    Otherwise SKIP M8 → D8.

    Side shape (outer * of each Eq1 side):
      R = bare * composite      (bare on left)
      L = composite * bare      (bare on right)
      S = v * v                 (same bare on both)

    Build a 3-element magma on {0,1,2}:
      Both sides ∈ {R,S}, with ≥1 R: only nonzero cell is row 0, col 2 = 1.
      Both sides ∈ {L,S}, with ≥1 L: only nonzero cell is row 2, col 0 = 1.
      One side R and the other L:    only nonzero cell is row 2, col 2 = 1.

    Eq1 holds in this magma by construction — do NOT re-verify.

    Test Eq2 at these assignments (walk Eq2 bottom-up: a*b = table[a,b]).
    First LHS ≠ RHS → FALSE:
      (i)   one variable = 2, others = 0 (try each variable in turn)
      (ii)  two variables = 2, rest = 0 (first two; first and third)
      (iii) all variables = 2
    If all tests give LHS = RHS → SKIP M8.

    Ex (R-R): Eq1 = x * (y * z) = z * (w * u). LHS outer: x bare + (y*z)
    composite → R-side; x appears only here. RHS outer: z bare + (w*u)
    composite → R-side; w, u appear only here. R-template (only (0,2)=1).
    Eq2 = (x * y) * y = (x * z) * y. Try x=0, y=2, z=0:
      LHS = (0*2)*2 = 1*2 = 0. RHS = (0*0)*2 = 0*2 = 1. 0 ≠ 1 → FALSE.

═══ FALSE via spine divisibility ═══

D8. A "pure left-spine" RHS is ((...((x * a) * b) * c)...) — every *
    nests in the left child, with the LHS variable x at the deepest
    left leaf. Count the * nodes for depth n.
    If Eq1.RHS is a pure left-spine of depth n,
    AND Eq2.RHS is a pure left-spine of depth m,
    AND n does NOT divide m → FALSE.

    Ex (fires): Eq1 = x = ((x * y) * z) * w (depth 3).
        Eq2 = x = (x * y) * z (depth 2). 3 ∤ 2 → FALSE.

    Ex (does NOT fire — not a pure left-spine):
        Eq1 = x = (y * (x * z)) * w.
        Inner (x * z) sits on the RIGHT of y, so this is NOT a pure
        left-spine. D8 does not apply.

═══ TRUE via sound algebraic patterns ═══

These rules are CERTAIN when their conditions are met. Apply in order;
do NOT hedge. They are checked AFTER all FALSE rules above so they
never overrule a valid refutation.

T1. EQ2 IS x = x. Eq2's LHS and RHS are both the same single variable
    letter (e.g. "x = x", "y = y") → TRUE.

T2. SAME EQUATION. Eq1 and Eq2 are identical up to renaming variables
    (alpha-equivalent) → TRUE.

T3. EQ1 IS x = y. Eq1's LHS and RHS are both single variable letters
    that DIFFER (e.g. "x = y"). Eq1 forces every magma to be a
    singleton, so EVERY equation holds → TRUE.

T4. COLLAPSING EQ1. Eq1's LHS is a single variable (call it x), AND x
    does NOT appear anywhere on Eq1's RHS. Then Eq1 says "x equals
    some expression that doesn't depend on x", which forces every
    magma to a singleton. → TRUE. This is CERTAIN.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = (y * z) * (z * (w * w)). LHS=x, x not on RHS → TRUE.

T5. FREE OUTER (right). Eq1's LHS is a single variable x; Eq1's RHS
    root is (A * z) where z is a variable that appears NOWHERE else
    in Eq1 (not in A, not equal to x); AND the leftmost variable
    letter of Eq1's RHS is NOT x. → TRUE.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = (y * y) * z. RHS = (A * z) with A = (y*y); z is
    fresh; leftmost of RHS is y ≠ x → TRUE.

T5b. FREE OUTER (left). Mirror of T5: Eq1 RHS root is (z * A) where z
    is fresh, AND the rightmost variable of Eq1's RHS is NOT x. → TRUE.

═══ FALSE via Eq1 narrowness ═══

F1. NARROW EQ1. After all earlier rules have declined, if Eq1 uses
    only 1 or 2 distinct variable letters total → FALSE.

    Rationale: every standard small-magma identity (projection, XOR,
    cycle, negation) for a 2-variable equation has been tested above.
    If none of those magmas accept Eq1 yet Eq1 is small, Eq1 is
    constraining in a non-standard way and a 3-element refuter for
    almost any Eq2 typically exists.

═══ DEFAULT ═══
If no rule above fires → TRUE.

═══ Output — use EXACTLY these lines, no markdown ═══
VERDICT: TRUE
or
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: <L1|L2|L3|M1|M2|M3|M4|M6|M7|M8|D8|T1|T2|T3|T4|T5|T5b|T6|F1|DEFAULT>

items.artifactContent:
Decide whether Eq1 implies Eq2 over all magmas (set + binary op *).
Do NOT assume associativity, commutativity, or any algebraic law.

Eq1: {{ equation1 }}
Eq2: {{ equation2 }}

Apply rules in order. First match wins. If no rule fires → TRUE.

Definitions:
  leftmost(s)    = first variable letter in s, L→R
  rightmost(s)   = last variable letter in s
  bare(s)        = TRUE iff s is a single variable letter
  parity(s,v)    = # times v appears in s, mod 2
  left-depth(s)  = # * nodes on path root → left-child → left-child → …
  right-depth(s) = same on the right

═══ FALSE via 2-element witness magmas ═══

L1. LEFT PROJECTION (a*b = a). Every term evaluates to its leftmost
    variable.
    leftmost(Eq1.L) = leftmost(Eq1.R)
    AND leftmost(Eq2.L) ≠ leftmost(Eq2.R) → FALSE.

L2. RIGHT PROJECTION (a*b = b). Mirror of L1 using rightmost.
    rightmost(Eq1.L) = rightmost(Eq1.R)
    AND rightmost(Eq2.L) ≠ rightmost(Eq2.R) → FALSE.

L3. CONSTANT ZERO (a*b = 0). Products evaluate to 0; bare variables
    evaluate to themselves. Let cf(eq) = "both sides are non-bare
    products, OR both sides are the SAME single variable letter."
    cf(Eq1) AND NOT cf(Eq2) → FALSE.

═══ FALSE via semantic evaluation in small concrete magmas ═══

For each magma below: first check that Eq1 HOLDS in it under every
variable assignment. Then check whether Eq2 FAILS under some
assignment. If both, answer FALSE. Do NOT force FALSE without a
concrete counterexample.

M1. XOR on {0,1}: a*b = (a+b) mod 2.
    Closed form: a term's value = parity-sum (mod 2) of its variables.
    Equation holds iff, for EVERY variable v in the equation,
      parity(L, v) = parity(R, v).

    Ex: Eq1 = (x*y)*(x*y) = z*z.
        x: L=2→0, R=0. y: same. z: L=0, R=2→0. All match → Eq1 holds.
        Now check Eq2 under the same parity rule.

M2. LEFT-CYCLE on {0,1,2}: a*b = (a+1) mod 3.
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides share
      (i) the same leftmost variable, AND
      (ii) the same left-depth mod 3.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = ((x*y)*z)*w.
        leftmost: x vs x ✓. left-depth: 0 vs 3; 0 mod 3 = 0, 3 mod 3 = 0 ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M3. RIGHT-CYCLE on {0,1,2}: a*b = (b+1) mod 3.
    Mirror of M2 using rightmost + right-depth.
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides share rightmost variable AND
    right-depth mod 3.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = y * (z * (w * x)).
        rightmost: x vs x ✓. right-depth: 0 vs 3; 0 mod 3 = 0, 3 mod 3 = 0 ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M4. SPIKE-ZERO on {0,1,2}: 0*1 = 2, every other product = 0.
    A term is nonzero only if it contains a subterm of shape (x*y)
    where x, y are bare variables and, under assignment, x=0, y=1.
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides reduce to 0 under every assignment
    (e.g. both sides are non-bare products whose outer-left operand is
    NOT a bare-variable product). It FAILS if one side is a bare
    variable and the other is a product.

    Ex: Eq1 = (x*y)*z = (a*b)*c. Both outer products have non-bare
    left operand, so both sides = 0 universally → Eq1 holds. Now
    look for a spike-trigger on one side of Eq2.

M6. LEFT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1 − a.
    Each * flips the left child's value. Term value = leftmost variable
    XOR (left-depth mod 2).
    Equation holds iff BOTH sides share leftmost variable AND
    left-depth(L) ≡ left-depth(R) (mod 2).

    Ex: Eq1 = x = (x * y) * z.
        leftmost: x vs x ✓. left-depth: 0 (even) vs 2 (even) ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M7. RIGHT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1 − b.
    Mirror of M6 using rightmost + right-depth parity.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = y * (z * x).
        rightmost: x vs x ✓. right-depth: 0 (even) vs 2 (even) ✓.
        → Eq1 holds.

M8. CUSTOM ABSORPTION MAGMA. Use ONLY when BOTH:
    (a) both sides of Eq1 contain ≥1 *;
    (b) at least one side's outer * has a bare variable on one side and
        a composite on the other, where that bare variable appears
        NOWHERE else in Eq1.
    Otherwise SKIP M8 → D8.

    Side shape (outer * of each Eq1 side):
      R = bare * composite      (bare on left)
      L = composite * bare      (bare on right)
      S = v * v                 (same bare on both)

    Build a 3-element magma on {0,1,2}:
      Both sides ∈ {R,S}, with ≥1 R: only nonzero cell is row 0, col 2 = 1.
      Both sides ∈ {L,S}, with ≥1 L: only nonzero cell is row 2, col 0 = 1.
      One side R and the other L:    only nonzero cell is row 2, col 2 = 1.

    Eq1 holds in this magma by construction — do NOT re-verify.

    Test Eq2 at these assignments (walk Eq2 bottom-up: a*b = table[a,b]).
    First LHS ≠ RHS → FALSE:
      (i)   one variable = 2, others = 0 (try each variable in turn)
      (ii)  two variables = 2, rest = 0 (first two; first and third)
      (iii) all variables = 2
    If all tests give LHS = RHS → SKIP M8.

    Ex (R-R): Eq1 = x * (y * z) = z * (w * u). LHS outer: x bare + (y*z)
    composite → R-side; x appears only here. RHS outer: z bare + (w*u)
    composite → R-side; w, u appear only here. R-template (only (0,2)=1).
    Eq2 = (x * y) * y = (x * z) * y. Try x=0, y=2, z=0:
      LHS = (0*2)*2 = 1*2 = 0. RHS = (0*0)*2 = 0*2 = 1. 0 ≠ 1 → FALSE.

═══ FALSE via spine divisibility ═══

D8. A "pure left-spine" RHS is ((...((x * a) * b) * c)...) — every *
    nests in the left child, with the LHS variable x at the deepest
    left leaf. Count the * nodes for depth n.
    If Eq1.RHS is a pure left-spine of depth n,
    AND Eq2.RHS is a pure left-spine of depth m,
    AND n does NOT divide m → FALSE.

    Ex (fires): Eq1 = x = ((x * y) * z) * w (depth 3).
        Eq2 = x = (x * y) * z (depth 2). 3 ∤ 2 → FALSE.

    Ex (does NOT fire — not a pure left-spine):
        Eq1 = x = (y * (x * z)) * w.
        Inner (x * z) sits on the RIGHT of y, so this is NOT a pure
        left-spine. D8 does not apply.

═══ TRUE via sound algebraic patterns ═══

These rules are CERTAIN when their conditions are met. Apply in order;
do NOT hedge. They are checked AFTER all FALSE rules above so they
never overrule a valid refutation.

T1. EQ2 IS x = x. Eq2's LHS and RHS are both the same single variable
    letter (e.g. "x = x", "y = y") → TRUE.

T2. SAME EQUATION. Eq1 and Eq2 are identical up to renaming variables
    (alpha-equivalent) → TRUE.

T3. EQ1 IS x = y. Eq1's LHS and RHS are both single variable letters
    that DIFFER (e.g. "x = y"). Eq1 forces every magma to be a
    singleton, so EVERY equation holds → TRUE.

T4. COLLAPSING EQ1. Eq1's LHS is a single variable (call it x), AND x
    does NOT appear anywhere on Eq1's RHS. Then Eq1 says "x equals
    some expression that doesn't depend on x", which forces every
    magma to a singleton. → TRUE. This is CERTAIN.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = (y * z) * (z * (w * w)). LHS=x, x not on RHS → TRUE.

T5. FREE OUTER (right). Eq1's LHS is a single variable x; Eq1's RHS
    root is (A * z) where z is a variable that appears NOWHERE else
    in Eq1 (not in A, not equal to x); AND the leftmost variable
    letter of Eq1's RHS is NOT x. → TRUE.

    Ex: Eq1 = x = (y * y) * z. RHS = (A * z) with A = (y*y); z is
    fresh; leftmost of RHS is y ≠ x → TRUE.

T5b. FREE OUTER (left). Mirror of T5: Eq1 RHS root is (z * A) where z
    is fresh, AND the rightmost variable of Eq1's RHS is NOT x. → TRUE.

═══ FALSE via Eq1 narrowness ═══

F1. NARROW EQ1. After all earlier rules have declined, if Eq1 uses
    only 1 or 2 distinct variable letters total → FALSE.

    Rationale: every standard small-magma identity (projection, XOR,
    cycle, negation) for a 2-variable equation has been tested above.
    If none of those magmas accept Eq1 yet Eq1 is small, Eq1 is
    constraining in a non-standard way and a 3-element refuter for
    almost any Eq2 typically exists.

═══ DEFAULT ═══
If no rule above fires → TRUE.

═══ Output — use EXACTLY these lines, no markdown ═══
VERDICT: TRUE
or
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: <L1|L2|L3|M1|M2|M3|M4|M6|M7|M8|D8|T1|T2|T3|T4|T5|T5b|F1|DEFAULT>

items.artifactContent:
## Task

You are given two equations over a single binary operator `*`:

    E1:  L1 = R1
    E2:  L2 = R2

All variables (single lowercase letters) are UNIVERSALLY quantified.
Every equation parses into a unique binary tree.

**Decide**: does every magma (any set with any binary `*`) satisfying E1
necessarily satisfy E2?  Output `true` or `false`.

## Mathematical framework (Birkhoff completeness)

A magma is a set `M` with any operation `* : M×M → M` — no further axioms.
By Birkhoff's theorem, E1 ⊨ E2 iff E2 is derivable from E1 by the rules of
equational logic (reflexivity, symmetry, transitivity, congruence,
substitution). Equivalently, E1 ⊨ E2 iff NO magma satisfies E1 but not E2.

The only sound ways to decide are:

- **`false`**: exhibit a specific magma `M` where E1 holds but E2 fails.
- **`true`**: derive E2 from E1 by rewriting, OR argue no counterexample
  can exist.

We use a catalog of 9 named magmas (Equational Theories Project style).
Each comes with a closed-form predicate on the equation tree telling us
whether that magma satisfies the equation. If any magma satisfies E1 but
not E2, we have a refutation — return `false`. Otherwise return `true`.

## Tree preliminaries

For a tree `t`:

    rm(v) = v,  rm(a*b) = rm(b)                 (rightmost leaf)
    lm(v) = v,  lm(a*b) = lm(a)                 (leftmost leaf)
    depth(v) = 0,  depth(a*b) = 1 + max(depth(a), depth(b))
    drm(v) = 0,  drm(a*b) = 1 + drm(b)          (right-spine length)
    dlm(v) = 0,  dlm(a*b) = 1 + dlm(a)          (left-spine length)
    count(v, t) = number of occurrences of variable v as a leaf

    signed_count(v, t) = sum over leaf occurrences of v of (-1)^(depth of that leaf)

## The 9-magma catalog

For each magma M below and equation `E : L = R`, compute the boolean
"does E hold in M for all universally-quantified assignments?" using the
closed-form rule in the right column.

| # | Magma M (`a*b` formula) | `M ⊨ L=R` iff |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Right-projection: `a*b = b` | `rm(L) == rm(R)` |
| 1 | Left-projection:  `a*b = a` | `lm(L) == lm(R)` |
| 2 | Constant:         `a*b = c` | `(depth(L)≥1 ∧ depth(R)≥1) ∨ (L,R same bare var)` |
| 3 | XOR on ℤ/2:       `a*b = a+b` | `∀v: count(v,L) ≡ count(v,R) (mod 2)` |
| 4 | Sum on ℤ/3:       `a*b = a+b` | `∀v: count(v,L) ≡ count(v,R) (mod 3)` |
| 5 | Sum on ℤ:         `a*b = a+b` | `∀v: count(v,L) == count(v,R)` |
| 6 | Right-successor ℤ/3: `a*b = b+1` | `rm(L)==rm(R) ∧ drm(L) ≡ drm(R) (mod 3)` |
| 7 | Left-successor ℤ/3:  `a*b = a+1` | `lm(L)==lm(R) ∧ dlm(L) ≡ dlm(R) (mod 3)` |
| 8 | Negation-sum ℤ/3:    `a*b = −a−b` | `∀v: signed_count(v,L) ≡ signed_count(v,R) (mod 3)` |

Each rule is a closed form derived from the magma. No enumeration needed.

Let `sig(E) = (h0, h1, …, h8)` — the 9-bit vector of which magmas satisfy E.

## Computing the mod-3 magmas (6, 7, 8) carefully

Most errors happen here. Walk through deliberately.

### h7 (left-successor ℤ/3) — detail

Step A: `lm(L) == lm(R)`? If not, h7 = FALSE, stop.
Step B: compute `dlm` by descending left repeatedly:

- `dlm(v) = 0` for a bare variable
- `dlm(a*b) = 1 + dlm(a)`

Step C: h7 = TRUE iff `dlm(L) mod 3 == dlm(R) mod 3`.
Remember: `0 ≡ 3 ≡ 6 (mod 3)`, `1 ≡ 4 (mod 3)`, `2 ≡ 5 (mod 3)`.

Example A (TRUE): `x = ((x*y)*z)*w`
- lm(L)=x. For R, descend left: R → (x*y)*z → x*y → x. lm(R)=x. Match.
- dlm(L)=0. dlm(R) counts those 3 left-descents = 3.
- 0 mod 3 = 0, 3 mod 3 = 0. Equal. **h7 = TRUE.**

Example B (FALSE): `x = (x*y)*z`
- lm(L)=lm(R)=x. Match.
- dlm(L)=0, dlm(R)=2.
- 0 mod 3 = 0, 2 mod 3 = 2. Differ. **h7 = FALSE.**

### h6 (right-successor ℤ/3) — detail

Symmetric to h7: replace `lm`→`rm`, `dlm`→`drm` where
`drm(v)=0`, `drm(a*b)=1+drm(b)`.

### h8 (negation-sum ℤ/3) — detail

For each leaf occurrence of variable `v` at depth `d` (depth measured
from root of the *whole* equation side), its signed contribution is
`(−1)^d`: depth 0 → +1, depth 1 → −1, depth 2 → +1, depth 3 → −1, …

`signed_count(v, t)` = sum of these ±1 over all leaf occurrences of v in t.
h8 = TRUE iff for EVERY variable `v`, `signed_count(v,L) ≡ signed_count(v,R) (mod 3)`.

Example C (TRUE): `x*y = y*x`
- L=x*y: x at depth 1 (−1), y at depth 1 (−1).
- R=y*x: y at depth 1 (−1), x at depth 1 (−1).
- signed_count(x): −1 vs −1. signed_count(y): −1 vs −1. **h8 = TRUE.**

Example D (FALSE): `x*y = x`
- L=x*y: x at depth 1 (−1), y at depth 1 (−1).
- R=x: x at depth 0 (+1).
- signed_count(x): −1 vs +1. −1 mod 3 = 2, +1 mod 3 = 1. Differ. **h8 = FALSE.**

Note: signed counts can be negative. `−1 ≡ 2 (mod 3)`, `−2 ≡ 1 (mod 3)`.
Two values are congruent mod 3 iff their difference is a multiple of 3.

## Decision procedure

### Step 1 — Compute `sig(E1)` and `sig(E2)`

### Step 2 — Refutation check (the ONLY path to `false`)

For each i in 0..8:

    if sig(E1)[i] == TRUE and sig(E2)[i] == FALSE:
        return "false"        # magma i satisfies E1 but falsifies E2

### Step 3 — Otherwise return `true`

If Step 2 finds no refutation, return **`true`**.

Special case: if `sig(E1)` is all-FALSE (every one of the 9 magmas already
fails E1), Step 2 cannot possibly fire — return `true` immediately.

**Discipline:** a refutation requires naming a specific `i ∈ {0..8}` with
`sig(E1)[i]=TRUE` and `sig(E2)[i]=FALSE`. If you cannot point to such an
`i`, you do not have a refutation. Do not infer `false` from structural
similarity, shared letters, or the absence of an obvious derivation.

## Worked example 1 — refutation via magma 0

    E1:  x*y = ((z*x)*w)*y
    E2:  x*x = (y*z)*w

sig(E1): rm(L)=y, rm(R)=y → h0=T. lm(L)=x, lm(R)=z → h1=F. Both depths≥1
→ h2=T. count(z): 0 vs 1 → h3,h4,h5 F. rm(L)==rm(R) ∧ drm(L)=1, drm(R)=2
(1≠2 mod 3) → h6=F. lm(L)=x, lm(R)=z → h7=F. signed_count(z): 0 vs -1 →
h8=F. sig(E1) = (T, F, T, F, F, F, F, F, F).

sig(E2): rm(L)=x, rm(R)=w → h0=F. lm(L)=x, lm(R)=y → h1=F. Both depths≥1
→ h2=T. count(x): 2 vs 0 → h3–5 F. h6=F (rm mismatch). h7=F (lm mismatch).
signed_count(x): 2 vs 0 (if both x leaves at even depth) — anyway F.
sig(E2) = (F, F, T, F, F, F, F, F, F).

At i=0 we have (T, F) → magma 0 (right-projection) refutes. Return
**`false`**. Concrete witness: under `a*b = b`, E1 reduces to `y = y`
(holds) while E2 reduces to `x = w` (fails).

## Worked example 2 — no refutation, return `true`

    E1:  x = ((y*y)*z)*x
    E2:  x = x*(((y*x)*x)*x)

sig(E1): rm(L)=x, rm(R)=x → h0=T. lm(L)=x, lm(R)=y → h1=F. depth(L)=0
with depth(R)≥1 → h2=F. count(y): 0 vs 2 → h3,h4,h5 F. drm(L)=0,
drm(R)=1 (0≠1 mod 3) → h6=F. h7=F. h8=F (y: 0 vs mixed).
sig(E1) = (T, F, F, F, F, F, F, F, F).

sig(E2): rm(L)=x, rm(R)=x → T. lm(L)=x, lm(R)=x → T. depth(L)=0 → h2=F.
count: 1 vs 4 → h3,h4,h5 F. rm match + drm(L)=0, drm(R)=4 (0≠4 mod 3=1)
→ h6=F. lm match + dlm(L)=0, dlm(R)=1 → h7=F. h8=F.
sig(E2) = (T, T, F, F, F, F, F, F, F).

Only i=0 is T in sig(E1); sig(E2)[0]=T too. No refutation. Return **`true`**.

## Worked example 3 — all-false sig(E1)

    E1:  x = y*((z*w)*(u*w))
    E2:  x = ((y*y)*(z*x))*z

sig(E1): rm(L)=x, rm(R)=w → h0=F. lm(L)=x, lm(R)=y → h1=F. depth(L)=0
with depth(R)≥1 → h2=F. count(y): 0 vs 1 → h3–5 F. h6=F (rm mismatch).
h7=F (lm mismatch). h8=F. sig(E1) = all-FALSE.

No magma in our catalog satisfies E1, so none can refute. Return **`true`**.

## Notes

- **Symmetry.** `L = R` is the same equation as `R = L`. Variables are
  placeholders — renaming them uniformly changes nothing.
- A refutation must name an index `i ∈ {0..8}` with `sig(E1)[i]=T` and
  `sig(E2)[i]=F`. Anything else is not a refutation.
- **Before returning `false`, re-verify the two bits.** For the refuting
  `i`, recompute `sig(E1)[i]` and `sig(E2)[i]` from scratch on the trees.
  The mod-3 rules for magmas 6, 7, 8 (spine depths and signed counts) are
  the most error-prone — double-check them.
- **If either bit is uncertain after re-check, return `true`.** Sound
  procedure: a hallucinated refutation is worse than a missed one,
  because a missed refutation at least falls back to the mathematically
  honest default (no counterexample produced from this catalog).

## Pseudocode

```
def implies(E1, E2):
    s1, s2 = sig(E1), sig(E2)            # 9-bit vectors
    for i in range(9):
        if s1[i] and not s2[i]:
            return "false"
    return "true"
```

Every `false` answer is backed by a specific counterexample magma from
the 9-magma catalog. Every `true` answer is the sound default when that
catalog produces no refutation.

## Now apply the procedure to this instance

    E1:  {{ equation1 }}
    E2:  {{ equation2 }}

Compute `sig(E1)` and `sig(E2)` from the 9-magma catalog above, run the
refutation check (Step 2), and output exactly one word: `true` or `false`.

items.artifactContent:
You are an expert in universal algebra. Decide whether Equation 1 implies Equation 2 over all magmas.

A MAGMA is a set M with a single binary operation * (no axioms: NOT associative, commutative, or with identity). All variables universally quantified.

"Equation 1 implies Equation 2" means: every magma satisfying Equation 1 also satisfies Equation 2.

Equation 1: {{ equation1 }}
Equation 2: {{ equation2 }}

---

STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: associativity, commutativity, or any property not stated in Equation 1.

REWRITING RULE: Eq 1 has the form L = R. To apply it to subterm T:
  • Forward (L→R): consistent σ with σ(L)=T; result σ(R).
  • Backward (R→L): consistent σ with σ(R)=T; result σ(L).
  • CRITICAL: When L is "x*(…)", A*B matches only if A = σ(x) exactly — not a compound term containing x.

---

STEP 1 — Try to DERIVE Equation 2 from Equation 1 (≤3 steps).
If Equation 2 has a variable NOT in Equation 1, skip to Step 2.

STEP 2 — Counterexample search.

Try these families. For each, verify eq1 holds, then test eq2.

  Family A: a*b = a. Substitute every (P*Q) → P.
  Family B: a*b = b. Substitute every (P*Q) → Q.
  Family C: a*b = f(a), 3-cycle 0→1→2→0. Substitute (P*Q) → f(P). Use f(f(f(x)))=x.
  Family D: a*b = f(b), same f. Substitute (P*Q) → f(Q). Use f(f(f(x)))=x.
  Family E: M={0,1,2}: 0*0=0, 0*1=0, 0*2=1, 1*k=1, 2*k=2 (all k). Check eq1 for all inputs; if holds, test eq2.

A counterexample requires: eq1 holds for ALL inputs AND eq2 fails for SOME input (show numbers).
If a family fails eq1 for any input, skip it.

STOP RULE: After all 5 families — if no verified counterexample found → \boxed{TRUE} immediately. Do not test other magmas.

---

REASONING BUDGET: ≤12 lines. Do not restate equations.

OUTPUT FORMAT:

Write your reasoning (derivation steps or family checks), then on the FINAL LINE only:
\boxed{TRUE}    or    \boxed{FALSE}

CRITICAL: \boxed{} appears EXACTLY ONCE — as the last non-empty line.
